title: PM Task Write Surface Integration Smoke
objective: Prove the new task POST and PATCH runtime path behaves correctly against a real PostgreSQL-backed environment by seeding the minimum supporting work rows, exercising create and partial update end to end, and confirming bounded validation, merged-error 422 handling, and 404 missing-row behavior without adding endpoints, changing schemas, or authoring new SQL.
active_role: PM Task Write Surface Integration Smoke Author
constrained_outputs: Real PostgreSQL-backed integration smoke coverage for the existing POST and PATCH task handlers

End-to-end evidence that the task write surface can create a row, patch a bounded subset of fields, reject known-bad work_package_id and primary_wbs_node_id references with the expected merged-error 422 response shape, and preserve 404 behavior for a missing task
Focused regression coverage proving packet-010a, packet-010b, packet-011f, packet-013, packet-013i, packet-013j, and packet-014 surfaces remain stable after the integration smoke addition
No database-schema changes, no new endpoints, and no broader write-side runtime implementation
validation_steps: Confirm packet 014i touches only integration-smoke coverage for the existing task POST and PATCH handlers and does not add new endpoints or broaden mutation scope

Confirm the live runtime path can create a task, patch a bounded subset of fields, return a merged-field 422 for known-bad work_package_id and primary_wbs_node_id references, and preserve 404 behavior for a missing task
Confirm the integration module mirrors the skip-when-unreachable posture used by packets 012g, 012i, and 013i

Confirm packet-010a model tests, packet-010b API tests, packet-011f project-write tests, packet-013 work-package write tests, packet-013i work-package integration smoke, packet-013j response enrichment tests, and packet-014 task-write tests still pass or skip as expected after the smoke coverage addition
sql_permission_note: Packet 014i is integration-smoke runtime coverage only. No new SQL, schema changes, migration edits, or broader write-surface expansion are authorized.
